concepts.description: Test without any assertion - test executes code but never verifies the outcome
detection_approaches: tree-sitter: detect test function nodes; traverse body to verify at least one assertion call exists
assertion calls: expect(), assert*, should, assertThat, pytest: assert statements
flag test functions where no assertion is found in body
false_positives: tests that return a value to be verified externally
tests that use mock verification instead of assertions
tests that expect exceptions (should use expect(() => ...).toThrow())

concepts.description: Test using time.sleep() - causes slow tests and flaky behavior
detection_approaches: tree-sitter: detect time.sleep() calls or equivalent in test functions
pattern match: sleep(duration), delay(), wait_for_seconds(), Thread.sleep()
flag any sleep > 0 in test context
false_positives: sleep in setUp/tearDown for resource cleanup timing
sleep in performance tests measuring timing behavior
sleeps in integration tests waiting for external services

concepts.description: Test skipped without explanation - @unittest.skip or describe.skip without reason
detection_approaches: tree-sitter: detect skip decorators with empty reason string
pattern: @skip, @skipIf, @skipUnless without message
JavaScript: describe.skip(), it.skip(), test.skip() with no explanation
false_positives: skip with detailed ticket number or explanation
skip conditionally based on environment variables
skip for known platform-specific issues

concepts.description: Test method not following naming convention - should start with test_
detection_approaches: tree-sitter: detect method definitions in test classes; verify name starts with 'test'
pattern: def test_*, async def test_*
flag methods in Test* classes that don't start with test
false_positives: helper methods that support tests (not test methods themselves)
setUp/tearDown methods
class methods like __init__

concepts.description: Test fixture setup too complex - setUp with >20 lines indicates high coupling
detection_approaches: tree-sitter: count lines in setUp/setUpClass fixture methods
flag setUp methods exceeding 20 lines of code
check for excessive mocking or complex object construction
false_positives: fixtures that initialize complex domain objects
database migration fixtures
integration test fixtures requiring multiple services

concepts.description: Multiple assertions in one test - test should have single logical assertion
detection_approaches: tree-sitter: count assertion statements in test function body
flag tests with >3 assertion calls
detect assertTrue/assertEqual chains
false_positives: assertions on related properties of same object
assertions in loops checking multiple elements
setup assertions that guard test prerequisites

concepts.description: Duplicated test method - identical or near-identical test code
detection_approaches: AST comparison: detect test functions with identical or highly similar bodies
similarity hashing of test function bodies
flag test methods with same assertions and same setup
false_positives: tests that verify same behavior with different inputs (parametrized tests)
tests with same intent but different assertions
tests for error handling vs success paths

concepts.description: Test using random values - creates non-deterministic/flaky tests
detection_approaches: tree-sitter: detect random.*, Math.random(), random.randint() calls in test body
flag use of random module, numpy.random, java.util.Random in tests
check for @RepeatedTest with random seed
false_positives: random used to generate test data that's then explicitly asserted
tests that use fixed seeds (random.seed(42))
property-based tests intentionally using random

concepts.description: Test without describe block - tests should be grouped in describe containers
detection_approaches: tree-sitter: detect it/test calls not nested inside describe blocks
flag root-level it(), test(), describe() without parent describe
check for test入れ子構造
false_positives: top-level integration tests
tests in __tests__ directories with descriptive file names
single test files that don't require grouping

concepts.description: expect.assertions() count mismatch - declared count doesn't match actual
detection_approaches: tree-sitter: detect expect.assertions(N) calls; count actual expect() calls in async test
dataflow: track assertion count for async tests with expect.assertions()
flag mismatch between declared and actual assertion count
false_positives: expect.assertions in test files that use only expect() without count verification
tests that use expect.hasAssertions() instead
conditional assertions based on platform/features

concepts.description: beforeAll/afterAll in nested describe - hooks should only be at top level
detection_approaches: tree-sitter: detect beforeAll/afterAll/beforeEach/afterEach in nested describe blocks
flag hooks inside describe blocks that are themselves nested
verify hook scope is only top-level
false_positives: hooks in nested describe for specific test groups
hooks for conditionally grouped tests
hooks in test classes with inheritance

concepts.description: mockImplementation used with mockReturnValue - should pick one
detection_approaches: tree-sitter: detect mock objects with both mockImplementation and mockReturnValue calls
flag same mock with conflicting return value specifications
check for .mockImplementation().mockReturnValue() chains
false_positives: different methods on same mock (one uses implementation, another uses return value)
mockImplementation for some calls, mockReturnValue for others
conditional mock behavior

concepts.description: spyOn with original implementation not restored - causes test pollution
detection_approaches: tree-sitter: detect spyOn calls without corresponding jest.restoreAllMocks()
track spyOn calls and verify cleanup in afterEach/tearDown
flag missing mock restoration pattern
false_positives: spyOn on jest.fn() mocks that auto-cleanup
mocks created with jest.spyOn + mockImplementation in same test
tests using jest.clearAllMocks() instead

concepts.description: act() wrapper missing for async state updates in React tests
detection_approaches: tree-sitter: detect async test functions that call setState/useState without act() wrapper
flag fireEvent/userEvent calls in async tests without act()
check for waitFor/waitForElementToBeRemoved without act()
false_positives: sync state updates that don't require act()
tests using @testing-library/react waitFor which handles act internally
enzyme shallow render tests

concepts.description: Weak assertion style - toBeTruthy vs toBe(true) causes ambiguity
detection_approaches: tree-sitter: detect expect(x).toBeTruthy() and suggest toBe(true) for boolean checks
flag toBeFalsy() that should be toBe(false)
detect assertTrue(x) that should assertEqual(x, expected)
false_positives: toBeTruthy for checking object existence
toBeFalsy for checking null/undefined
assertions on functions that return boolean-like values
